However, another interpretation may argue that the final license requires at least disclosure, though not always inclusion, of the complete corresponding source code which enables a sufficiently well trained engineer to extract the differences, understand the changes and derive a summary which then would be, at least partly, in line with the PSF-2.0 license requirement.

Interpretation: The requirement of the Python license to provide a brief summary of the changes may definitely be seen as a further restriction the final license prohibits to impose, since it requires to describe the changes rather than only to mention the fact that changes were made and such brief summary must be included in source code and binary delivery. However, another interpretation may argue that the final license requires at least disclosure, though not always inclusion, of the complete corresponding source code which enables a sufficiently well trained engineer to extract the differences, understand the changes and derive a summary which then would be, at least partly, in line with the Python license requirement.

In addition, a "license that complies with this license" may or may not be interpreted as a copyleft clause. If not, the copyleft condition would depend on the mode of delivery which would require a case-by-case assessment. Therefore, a general recommendation on the compatibility of the MS-PL license can only be given for selected cases. MS-PL.explanation: It is unclear whether "any portion of the software" assumes code changes and additions that also must be distributed "only under this license". In addition, a "license that complies with this license" may or may not be interpreted as a copyleft clause. If not, the copyleft condition would depend on the mode of delivery which would require a case-by-case assessment. Therefore, a general recommendation on the compatibility of the MS-PL license can only be given for selected cases. Interpretation: It is unclear whether "any portion of the software" assumes code changes and additions that also must be distributed "only under this license". OpenSSL.explanation: The final statement of the appended SSLeay License "The licence and distribution terms for any publically available version or derivative of this code cannot be changed" normally must be interpreted as a copyleft clause, but there is no general consensus on this interpretation. Therefore, a recommendation on the compatibility of the OpenSSL license can only be given for selected cases.
